questions.prompt: Transform to passive: "They make these shoes in Italy." The correct passive is:

options.text: These shoes are made in Italy.
These shoes were made in Italy.
These shoes is made in Italy.
These shoes are make in Italy.

questions.explanation: Active: present simple. "These shoes" is plural → ARE + past participle of make (made): These shoes are made in Italy.

questions.prompt: Transform to passive: "The teacher corrected our homework." The correct passive is:

options.text: Our homework is corrected by the teacher.
Our homework were corrected by the teacher.
Our homework was correct by the teacher.
Our homework was corrected by the teacher.

questions.explanation: Active verb is past simple (corrected). "Our homework" is singular → WAS + past participle (corrected): Our homework was corrected by the teacher.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence where the passive is used correctly:

options.text: The pyramids were build thousands of years ago.
The pyramids were built thousands of years ago.
The pyramids was built thousands of years ago.
The pyramids are built thousands of years ago.

questions.explanation: "The pyramids" is plural + past time → WERE + past participle of build (built): The pyramids were built thousands of years ago.

questions.prompt: Active: "A student finds a wallet." → Passive:

options.text: A wallet is found by a student.
A wallet found by a student.
A wallet were found by a student.
A wallet was found by a student.

questions.explanation: Active verb is present simple (finds). "A wallet" is singular → IS + past participle of find (found): A wallet is found by a student.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly OMITS the by-agent because it is obvious:

options.text: The patient was examined by a doctor.
The patient is examining.
The patient was examined.
The patient examines.

questions.explanation: When it is obvious that a doctor performs the examination, "by a doctor" adds no information and is correctly omitted: The patient was examined.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence with the correct passive: "The new library ___ last year." (open)

options.text: is opened
was opened
opened
were opened

questions.explanation: "The new library" is singular and "last year" signals past tense → WAS + past participle (opened): The new library was opened last year.
